5: ## Cline-CLI adapter — V1 (binary swap) + V2 (cross-mount stage-mirror) shipped (2026-06-06)
6: 
7: **Shipped:** Two operator-side validation fixes following the v1
8: adapter ship — same V1/V2 follow-up arc the Hermes adapter
9: established as the closing step for a new-adapter build.
10: 
11: ### V1 — running daemon predated the adapter (operational, not code)
12: 
13: **Symptom (operator-reported):** "I have just installed and run the
14: Cline CLI from WSL. Can you please check why none of those sessions
15: are being captured by our observer, because I don't see the session
16: or the messages or any of the other details being captured?"
17: 
18: **Diagnosis:** the WSL2 observer daemon was `bin/observer-hermes`
19: mod-time Jun 6 01:30 — predated all 13 clinecli commits which
20: started landing later the same day. The binary literally didn't
21: have the cline-cli adapter compiled in. observer.db had zero
22: `cline-cli` rows; daemon log had zero `cline-cli` adapter poll
23: entries.
24: 
25: **Fix:** rebuild observer with the current clinecli code → `kill
26: -TERM` the old daemon process → swap `bin/observer-hermes` in
27: place → restart from the same path so the `wslrelay.exe` PID 33168
28: port forwarding kept working without re-config. Pre-V1 backup at
29: `bin/observer-hermes.pre-clinecli` for rollback.
30: 
31: **Verification:** post-swap the WSL session `1780734869176_j3zh2`
32: (minimax/minimax-m3) landed cleanly with 22 actions (1 session_start
33: + 1 session_end + 7 user_prompt + 7 task_complete + 4 run_command
34: + 1 edit_file + 1 web_fetch) + 14 token_usage rows (1 session-level
35: + per-message metrics from messages.json — the Phase 0 reality-check
36: upgrade working as designed).
37: 
38: ### V2 — cross-mount SQLite stage-mirror (commit `aee05890`)
39: 
40: **Symptom (operator-reported, second pass):** "I have just tried
41: client CLI on Windows, and I've entered a few messages, but I don't
42: see them being reflected in the dashboard."
43: 
44: **Diagnosis:** the daemon log had been spamming every scan loop:
45: 
46: ```
47: WARN watcher.Scan: process failed adapter=cline-cli
48:      path=/mnt/c/Users/marmu/.cline/data/db/sessions.db
49:      err="clinecli.ParseSessionFile: clinecli.scanStateDB:
50:           readSessions: query: disk I/O error (4618)"
51: ```
52: 
53: — 18 cumulative WARN entries (3 paths × 6 scan loops) without ever
54: advancing the parse cursor for the Windows-mount path. Three live
55: Windows-side sessions sitting unread in
56: `C:\Users\marmu\.cline\data\db\sessions.db`:
57: `1780701711502_0v8d2` (failed, deepseek), `1780738426967_uwpa6`
58: (idle, deepseek), `1780738491538_qs3wx` (idle, deepseek). Root
59: cause: modernc.org/sqlite returns `SQLITE_IOERR_SEEK` (4618) when
60: opening a `/mnt/c/`-rooted sessions.db while the Windows cline
61: process holds the WAL open — WSL's DrvFs bridge translates the FD
62: into something SQLite's `pread`/seek can't traverse.
63: 
64: **Fix** (`internal/adapter/clinecli/mirror.go`, ~140 LOC + 4 test
65: functions / 13 sub-cases): the stage-mirror pattern opencode
66: adopted for the identical Windows-side OpenCode Desktop problem
67: (`internal/adapter/opencode/adapter.go::stageMirrorIfForeign`).
68: 
69: - `stageMirrorIfForeign(srcDB)`: native paths pass through with
70:   zero overhead (test:
71:   `TestStageMirrorIfForeign_NativePassThrough`). Foreign-mount
72:   paths get the SQLite trio (`.db` + `-wal` + `-shm`) copied via
73:   `os.ReadFile` / `os.WriteFile` (both work fine over `/mnt/c/`) to
74:   `<UserCacheDir>/superbased-observer/clinecli-mirror/<sha8>/sessions.db`
75:   with a per-source hash so concurrent WSL + Windows installs
76:   don't trample each other.
77: - `mirrorUpToDate`: skip the copy when the WAL mtime hasn't
78:   advanced — Cline flushes the WAL on every turn, so its sibling
79:   check is the fast-moving signal. Size guard catches in-flight
80:   truncate/realloc that mtime alone misses.
81: - `isForeignMountPath`: walks `crossmount.AllHomes()` looking for a
82:   non-native home whose path is a prefix of the input.
83: - Missing-sibling handling: `-wal` and `-shm` get removed from the
84:   mirror when the source's are gone — Cline checkpointing the WAL
85:   must not leave a stale mirror sibling shadowing the freshly-
86:   checkpointed source.
87: 
88: `statedb.go::scanStateDB` routes `dbPath` through
89: `stageMirrorIfForeign` before opening. The change is exactly 4
90: lines of statedb.go on top of ~140 LOC of mirror.go.
91: 
92: **Verification** (post-swap + `observer backfill --clinecli-rescan`):
93: 
94: | Metric | Before V2 | After V2 |
95: |---|---:|---:|
96: | cline-cli WARN entries per scan | 3 (~18 cumulative) | 0 |
97: | Sessions captured | 1 (WSL only) | 4 (1 WSL + 3 Windows) |
98: | Actions | 22 | 44 |
99: | Token rows | 14 | 25 |
100: 
101: The two newly-captured Windows `idle`-status sessions correctly
102: don't emit `session_end` (the `terminalStatus` map excludes `idle`
103: + `running`). The yesterday-old `failed` session does emit
104: `session_end` with `Target="failed"`.

135: ## Cline-CLI adapter — full v1 shipped through Phase 3 (docs) (2026-06-06)
136:

138: the Phase 0 fixture capture through Phase 3 user docs. ~3,500 LOC
139: Go + Python + docs across 12 logical commits.
140: 
141: **Architecture is SQLite-primary + optional hooks tail** (statedb +
142: messages.json walker as the canonical path; hooks.jsonl as a
143: real-time accelerator when the operator has registered hook
144: scripts). The SQLite path reads from `~/.cline/data/db/sessions.db`
145: (schema v1, 28-column sessions table) + per-session
146: `<id>.messages.json` files. The hook path tails
147: `~/.cline/data/logs/hooks.jsonl` past a byte-offset cursor.
148: 
149: **Phase 0 reality-check upgrades vs. the plan:**
150: 
151: - **Per-message `metrics` block exists** — plan §4/§7 had it as
152:   session-level-only. Per-message TokenEvents emit via Tier 2
153:   approximate.
154: - **Per-message `modelInfo` block** — `{id, provider, family?}`
155:   overrides session-level model on per-message rows so mid-session
156:   provider switches attribute correctly.
157: - **`tool_result.content` is a list of `{query, result, success,
158:   error}` dicts** — structured shape, decoded by
159:   `decodeToolResultContent` with a bare-string fallback.
160: - **`sessions.cwd` is always populated** — drives ProjectRoot
161:   resolution directly via `normalizeProjectRoot` →
162:   `crossmount.TranslateForeignPath` (mirrors hermes H2; defends
163:   against Windows-on-WSL2 CWD-prefix-onto-observer's-own-repo).
164: - **`<user_input mode="...">` wrapper** stripped via regex before
165:   emitting user_prompt rows on both SQLite + hook paths.
166: - **`team_name` populated on non-team sessions** — surfaced to
167:   ActionMetadata as observability metadata, NOT used as a sub-
168:   agent signal.
169: - **`secrets.json` discovery** — added to plan Appendix B + the
170:   testdata README + IsSessionFile's reject list.
171: 
172: **Operator surface** (`observer backfill --clinecli-rescan`): walks
173: every `sessions.db` under the cline-cli watch roots (~/.cline/data/db/)
174: from `updated_at='0'` and re-emits all events. Idempotent via the
175: UNIQUE(source_file, source_event_id) constraint. Also included in
176: `--all`.
177: 
178: **Architecturally distinct from `internal/adapter/cline/`** (the
179: VS Code Cline extension adapter): same brand, different products.
180: Naming decision: additive, no rename, matching the
181: `ToolCopilot`+`ToolCopilotCLI` precedent. The constants
182: `models.ToolCline = "cline"` (VSCode extension) and
183: `models.ToolClineCLI = "cline-cli"` (CLI) live side-by-side with
184: cross-referencing doc-comments.

186: **Cross-path dedup gate**: `SessionHookChecker` wired into Adapter
187: (`WithSessionHookChecker(check)`) so the SQLite path skips
188: sessions the hook path has already covered — defends against the

190: operational workaround (run one path at a time). cline-cli ships

363: ## Hermes adapter — V4 fix: bake observer binary path into plugin at install time (2026-06-06)
364: 
365: **Cross-platform validation arc**: ran the operator-side end-to-end against the live `%LOCALAPPDATA%\hermes\state.db` on Windows-native after the WSL2-only validation that landed V1/V2/V3. SQLite backfill path landed clean on Windows: schema_version=14, 1 session / 23 actions (including the V3 system_prompt + 6 user_prompt + 7 task_complete rows) / 1 token_usage row. Schema layout matches WSL exactly (the adapter reads from `schema_version` table, not `meta`). `observer init --hermes` correctly targets `%LOCALAPPDATA%\hermes\plugins\superbased-observer\` and the `mcp_servers` + `plugins.enabled` writers merge into `config.yaml` cleanly. `hermes plugins list` shows `superbased-observer  enabled  user`.
366: 
367: **Bug surfaced (V4)**: running a Hermes prompt did NOT cause hook events to land. Diagnosis: the embedded `__init__.py` defaulted `OBSERVER_BIN = os.environ.get("OBSERVER_BIN", "observer")` — a bare PATH lookup. On Windows `observer` on PATH resolves to the npm shim (`%APPDATA%\npm\observer.cmd`) which requires Node; without Node the subprocess fails silently and every hook event is dropped because the plugin swallows all subprocess exceptions on purpose. The plugin's own docstring already promised `observer init --hermes` would substitute the resolved binary path at install time, but no substitution machinery existed — `HermesOptions.BinaryPath` was already populated by the caller (`cmd/observer/init.go::absoluteBinaryPath` via `os.Executable()`) but `RegisterHermes` ignored it.
368: 
369: **Fix**:
370: - `internal/hook/hermesplugin/__init__.py:55` — replaced the `"observer"` literal with the `"{{OBSERVER_BIN_DEFAULT}}"` placeholder. Docstring (line 18-25) updated to describe the new install-time substitution.
371: - `internal/hook/hermesplugin/embed.go` — `WritePlugin(destDir, defaultObserverBin string)` now substitutes the placeholder via `bytes.Replace` (n=1) with the Python-escaped binary path. New `escapePythonStringContents` helper handles backslash + double-quote + newline / tab escapes so Windows paths render as valid Python string literals (`r""` raw-strings can't end in a single backslash, so we go with explicit-escape). Empty `defaultObserverBin` falls back to bare `"observer"` for back-compat with tests.
372: - `internal/hook/hermes.go::RegisterHermes` — now forwards `opts.BinaryPath` to `WritePlugin`. The Options.BinaryPath docstring was already correct; this just makes the implementation match.
373: - `internal/hook/hermesplugin/embed_test.go` — three existing call-sites migrated to `WritePlugin(dir, "")`. New `TestWritePlugin_SubstitutesObserverBinDefault` covers four scenarios: Linux abs path, Windows path with backslashes, embedded double-quote, and the empty-fallback default.
374: 
375: **Live verification on the operator's Windows host**: with `$env:OBSERVER_BIN` explicitly cleared, the rendered plugin lands `OBSERVER_BIN = os.environ.get("OBSERVER_BIN", "D:\\programsx\\superbased-observer\\bin\\observer-hermes.exe")` on disk. Running `hermes -z "Reply with the single word: WORKS"` produces a new session whose `session_start` + `session_end` + `post_api_request` (1 token row) events ALL arrive via `source_file='hermes:hook'`. Tested against the live Hermes install at `%LOCALAPPDATA%\hermes\hermes-agent\venv\Scripts\hermes.exe`. Python parser on the rendered file: clean `ast.parse` pass.
376: 
377: **Why fixture tests missed V4 (parallel to why fixture tests missed V1/V2)**: `embed_test.go` only verified that `WritePlugin` writes both files. It didn't exercise an end-to-end hermes session against the plugin. The validation now lives in `docs/hermes-adapter.md` "Validating an install end-to-end" as a published prerequisite for shipping any future change to the hook subprocess invocation path.
378: 
379: **V4 is universal — same root cause exists on Linux/macOS/WSL2.** The WSL2 hook path "worked" earlier in this session likely because either (a) Windows-side npm shims happen to function via WSL2 interop even though node isn't installed there, or (b) the operator's shell rc happened to export `OBSERVER_BIN`. Either way the install was fragile; V4 makes it deterministic on every platform.

459: **Architecture is hooks-primary + SQLite backfill** (cursor adapter precedent). Hook path: Python plugin at `~/.hermes/plugins/superbased-observer/` registers `ctx.register_hook("post_tool_call", …)` / `"post_api_request"` / `"on_session_start"` / `"on_session_end"` / `"subagent_stop"` and fires `observer hook hermes <event>` as fire-and-forget subprocess (0.5s timeout, exceptions swallowed). SQLite path: `internal/adapter/hermes/statedb.go` opens `~/.hermes/state.db` read-only via `modernc.org/sqlite`, filters `messages.active = 1`, walks past last-seen `messages.id` watermark. Dedup via `(source_file, source_event_id)` UNIQUE — hook rows carry `"hermes:hook"`, backfill rows carry the absolute state.db path.

463: - `messages.active = 1` filter required — `active=0` marks rewound/compressed-out rows that must NOT emit events. Sentinel test (`TestScanStateDB_FiltersActiveZero`) guards regression.

465: - `ctx.register_hook(hook_name, callback)` API confirmed at `hermes_cli/plugins.py:938`. Plugin auto-loads from `~/.hermes/plugins/<name>/` — no `config.yaml.plugins.enabled` entry needed.
